description:
A developer is tidying up a small project-level “firewall” configuration that lives completely inside the workspace (it is **not** the system firewall and does not require root rights).  
  The project folder already contains  
    /home/user/project/security/firewall_rules.conf  

  Right now that file holds exactly two lines (line-break is Unix “\n”):  
    22/tcp ALLOW  
    80/tcp ALLOW  

  The developer has started a local HTTP development server that listens on TCP port 3000 and wants the project’s internal policy file to allow it.  
  Your task is to append one single rule line to the very end of the existing file so that it becomes three lines in total, with the new rule formatted **exactly** like this (make sure to keep the forward-slash and the single space before “ALLOW”):  

    3000/tcp ALLOW  

  After updating the file, produce a tiny status log called  
    /home/user/project/security/firewall_update.log  

  The log must contain **exactly four lines**, with a trailing newline at the end of the fourth line. The required content is:

    Update successful  
    Current rules:  
    22/tcp ALLOW  
    80/tcp ALLOW  
    3000/tcp ALLOW  

  • Line-1: literal text “Update successful”  
  • Line-2: literal text “Current rules:”  
  • Lines-3-to-5: the three rules now present inside firewall_rules.conf, in the same order as they appear in that file.  
  • Each line must end with a Unix newline character “\n”.

  A single, well-chosen terminal command is enough to finish the whole job (append the rule and then create the log).  
  Do not rename, move or delete any existing file; just append the rule and generate the new log file in the same directory.  
  When you are finished, both files should be readable with ordinary user permissions.
truth:
Initial workspace (already present before agent starts):

    Directory: /home/user/project/security/
      ├── firewall_rules.conf
      └── (nothing else)

  Content of /home/user/project/security/firewall_rules.conf before the agent touches it:
    22/tcp ALLOW\n80/tcp ALLOW\n    (exactly; ends with a newline)

  Expected final state after the agent completes:

    /home/user/project/security/firewall_rules.conf
      Content (including trailing newline):
        22/tcp ALLOW\n80/tcp ALLOW\n3000/tcp ALLOW\n

    /home/user/project/security/firewall_update.log
      Content (including trailing newline):
        Update successful\nCurrent rules:\n22/tcp ALLOW\n80/tcp ALLOW\n3000/tcp ALLOW\n

  Both files must have regular user-writable permissions (0600–0666 range).
name: task_000000_6a3525e8
